---SECTION 2: TOP-3 REGIMEN OPTIONS---
Option 1: Switch to valproate-based therapy (remove carbamazepine)
  - carbamazepine: stop
  - phenobarbital: continue
  - valproate: start
  Rationale: Ongoing twice-daily generalized convulsions despite CBZ+PB suggests the regimen is not appropriately matched; starting valproate (broad-spectrum, cost-effective) while continuing PB during transition offers the best chance of rapid control, with later simplification if improved.

Option 2: Valproate monotherapy (simplify to one appropriate anchor)
  - carbamazepine: stop
  - phenobarbital: stop
  - valproate: start
  Rationale: If safe to transition and close follow-up is feasible, moving to valproate monotherapy prioritizes an appropriate generalized-seizure anchor and reduces sedation/interaction burden from dual older enzyme-inducing therapy.

Option 3: If valproate is unsuitable/unavailable—phenobarbital optimization with clobazam adjunct
  - carbamazepine: stop
  - phenobarbital: continue
  - clobazam: start
  Rationale: When valproate cannot be used, PB remains an accessible backbone and clobazam is a practical adjunct for breakthrough generalized convulsions while avoiding adding a third long-term interacting antiseizure agent.
